name: prettier
usage_summary: Used in one production file in the 'app' package: packages/app/src/cli/utilities/file-formatter.ts exports a single `formatContent()` function that calls `prettier.format(content, options)`. That function is called in exactly one place: packages/app/src/cli/models/extensions/specifications/ui_extension.ts to format generated TypeScript type definitions. In 'eslint-plugin-cli', prettier is listed as a devDependency but only `eslint-config-prettier` is required in source (config.js); prettier itself is not directly imported.
how_to_remove: The single production use is formatting generated TypeScript source with options {parser: 'typescript', singleQuote: true}. This could be replaced by: (a) dropping formatting of generated types entirely (lowest effort, output is consumed programmatically), or (b) switching to a lighter alternative such as `dprint` (wasm-based, much smaller install) or simply calling the TypeScript compiler's printer API. Either path touches one wrapper file (file-formatter.ts) and one call site (ui_extension.ts). The eslint-plugin-cli devDependency can be removed without any source change.
replacement_reason: The sole production use is formatting auto-generated TypeScript that is not shown to users, so dropping formatting is the simplest path. If formatting must be preserved, dprint or @typescript-eslint/parser's built-in printer are lighter alternatives. The eslint-plugin-cli devDependency is already unused in source.
